vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on gshadow File
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/gshadow, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0000 /etc/gshadow

triage_decision.reason: This finding involves setting standard file permissions on /etc/gshadow, which is a critical system file for group password management. The remediation follows standard STIG hardening patterns and does not involve changes that would cause lockout or system breakage.
